title: Within-lap ranking sidecar for pit imminence
summary: F1 strategy is relative: official analysis says undercut/overcut decisions depend on rival position, traffic, and being within striking range, often around two seconds.
rationale: F1 strategy is relative: official analysis says undercut/overcut decisions depend on rival position, traffic, and being within striking range, often around two seconds. That implies the model should learn who is most pit-likely within each `Year-Race-LapNumber` snapshot, not only independent binary risk. A LambdaMART/YetiRank sidecar is a concrete way to inject this competition structure.
implementation_hint: Create query groups by `Year, Race, LapNumber`. Train a ranking model with `PitNextLap` as relevance using LightGBM `lambdarank` or CatBoost ranking. Use its OOF score as a feature or blend it with the binary classifier by rank-averaging or logistic stacking.
expected_effect: Better ordering among cars sharing the same strategic window; likely small but real AUC lift from capturing relative pit priority the current absolute-feature model misses.
risk: Ranking objectives are not probability-calibrated and may underperform if many lap groups contain no positives or multiple simultaneous positives.
